DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF ARMANDO REYES' MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING DEFENDANT STARBUCKS CORPORATION AND JEN LU TO FURTHER RESPOND TO PLAINTIFF'S FORM INTERROGATORIES. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION OF PLAINTIFF ARMANDO REYES TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SATISFACTORILY ESTABLISH HE MET THE STATUTORY MEET AND CONFER REQUIREMENTS PRIOR TO FILING THE MOTION. A MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES MUST BE ACCOMPANIED BY A MEET AND CONFER DECLARATION THAT STATES FACTS SHOWING THE MOVING PARTY MADE A REASONABLE AND GOOD FAITH ATTEMPT AT AN INFORMAL RESOLUTION OF EACH ISSUE PRESENTED BY THE MOTION. (CCP 2016.040, 2030.300.) THE SANCTIONS REQUESTS OF BOTH PARTIES ARE DENIED. HOWEVER, THE COURT ADMONISHES PLAINTIFF TO FAMILIARIZE HIMSELF WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF DISCOVERY MOTION PRACTICE TO AVOID THE RISK OF INCURRING SANCTIONS IN THE FUTURE DUE TO NONCOMPLIANCE WITH THE RULES CONCERNING SUCH MOTIONS. (SEE E.G., CCP 2023.010 (FAILURE TO CONFER IS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS) AND CCP 2023.020 (MONETARY SANCTIONS FOR FAILURE TO CONFER).) ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE PRO TEM: PETER VESTAL, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING ON PLAINTIFF ARMANDO REYES' DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT, AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF REYES' DEMURRER TO ANSWER IS OFF CALENDAR. FIRST, THE DEMURRER IS UNTIMELY UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, SEC. 430.40(B) ["[A] PARTY WHO HAS FILED A COMPLAINT ... MAY, WITHIN 10 DAYS AFTER SERVICE OF THE ANSWER TO HIS PLEADING, DEMUR TO THE ANSWER."] HERE, DEFENDANTS SERVED THE ANSWER ON APRIL 8, 2019 AND PLAINTIFF FILED THE DEMURRER ON MAY 16, 2019. SECOND, AND MORE IMPORTANTLY, THERE IS NO DECLARATION SHOWING THAT THE PARTIES MET AND CONFERRED REGARDING THE ALLEGED DEFECTS IN THE ANSWER BEFORE THIS DEMURRER WAS FILED. (SEE CODE CIV. PROC., SEC. 430.41.) THE COURT SIGNED THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT STARBUCKS, AS REQUIRED BY THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: ETHAN P. MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Dec-08-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Video Conferencing
Armando Reyes-Sanft, 209-596-3023, Plaintiff in Pro Per
Emily Genge, Esq., , Burnham Brown, 510-444-6800, for Defendant(s) Starbucks Corporation, Jen Lu

Court addresses plaintiff Armando Reyes-Sanft's 170.6 premptory challenge. Court states the the 170.6 challenge was filed untimely and therefore is denied. Plaintiffs ex parte application to continued today's hearing did not comply with the rules of court re: Ex parte application. Plaintiff did not appear ex parte on December 7, 2020. Court states that plaintiff's opposition filed on December 4, 2020 is untimely and can not be considered. No opposition filed. Court states to plaintiff that there is not an order excusing plantiff from untimely filings on record. Court address plaintiff's 170.1 request for recusal. Plaintiff's 170.1 request for recusal is Denied.

ARGUED. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows

DEFENDANTs KEVIN JOHNSON, PATRICK GRISMER, and PETER FILIPOVIC's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS

Granted. No opposition filed. Order signed. Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/EPS)

Status Conference: Discovery (Special Set Per Order Filed 8/28/20)
Off Calendar Oct-28-2020 Off Calendar

Defendants Starbucks Corporation, And Jen Lu'S Motion To Set Aside The Default Of Corporate Officers Kevin Johnson, Patrick Grismer And Peter Filipovic Entered On February 10, 2020 (Per Ex Parte Order Filed 10-20-2020, Fully Briefed, Previously Set For 3-27-2020)
Off Calendar Oct-29-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance is made in this matter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendants Starbucks Corporation, And Jen Lu'S Motion To Set Aside The Default Of Corporate Officers Kevin Johnson, Patrick Grismer And Peter Filipovic Entered On February 10, 2020 is GRANTED. The record is undisputed that Plaintiff took the default of the corporate officers although he knew they were represented by counsel, who had informed him-twice-that they did not believe the officers had been properly served, and that counsel would be entering an appearance on their behalf. (Genge Decl., 11, 12 & Exs. D and E.) Plaintiff also ignored Defendants' offer to meet and confer regarding the adequacy of service. Plaintiff's conduct was unprofessional and unethical. (LaSalle v. Vogel (2019) 36 Cal.App.5th 127, 135 ["it is now well acknowledged that an attorney has an ethical obligation to warn counsel that the attorney is about to take an adversary's default"].) In Defendants' second letter, on January 30, 2020, Defendants explicitly put Plaintiff on notice that if he attempted to take the individuals' default, Defendants would move to set aside the defaults and seek monetary sanctions. Further, by order filed March 4, 2020, the Court granted an effectively identical motion to set aside the default of another of Defendants' employees, yet Plaintiff has not stipulated to set aside these defaults. The Court warned Plaintiff in its March 4 order that "if he continues to engage in unprofessional and unethical conduct, to make frivolous arguments, and to overburden the Court and the parties with late and unmeritorious filings, the Court will not hesitate to impose substantial sanctions on its own motion or on motion by Defendants." Plaintiff having ignored Defendants' and the Court's warnings, that time has come. The Court awards sanctions against Plaintiff in the amount of $2,150.00, which shall be paid within 10 days of this order.

Written order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: proceedings not reported.

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Mar-04-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Emily G. Genge, Esq. of BURNHAM BROWN, T:510.835.6819, for defendant/moving party;
Armando Reyes, T:209.596.3023, plaintiff appearing on his own behalf, by Court Call.

RULING: Mr. Reyes argues the Court's tentative ruling. After argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS STARBUCKS CORPORATION AND JEN LU'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT, as follows:

Defendants' motion to set aside entry of default of Scott Carter entered on December 31, 2019 is granted. Plaintiff Armando Reyes took Mr. Carter's default without prior notice to his counsel, although he knew Mr. Carter was represented by counsel. That action was unprofessional and unethical. (LaSalle v. Vogel (2019) 36 Cal.App.5th 127, 135 ["it is now well acknowledged that an attorney has an ethical obligation to warn counsel that the attorney is about to take an adversary's default"].) Plaintiff's ridiculously voluminous declaration in response to the motion, which comprises literally an entire box of exhibits, is untimely and fails to rebut defendant's factual showing on that point. Plaintiff has not filed a memorandum of points and authorities in opposition to the motion. Plaintiff's legal argument, which he improperly includes in his declaration, is that in order for a party to be granted relief from default under Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b), the motion "must be" accompanied by an attorney affidavit of fault. (Reyes Decl. Para. 6.) Plaintiff is wrong. Section 473(b) consists of two distinct parts: "a discretionary provision, which applies permissively, and a mandatory provision, which applies as of right." (Minick v. City of Petaluma (2016) 3 Cal.App.5th 15, 25-26.) An attorney affidavit of fault is required only for the latter. (Code Civ. Proc. 473(b).)

Plaintiff is warned that if he continues to engage in unprofessional and unethical conduct, to make frivolous arguments, and to overburden the Court and the parties with late and unmeritorious filings, the Court will not hesitate to impose substantial sanctions on its own motion or on motion by Defendants.

Additionally, the Court ordered Mr. Reyes to effect service on defendant Carter through Carter's counsel.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Temporarily Stay All Legal Proceedings Pending The Resolution Ot The Emergency Appeal By California Court Of Appeals
Off Calendar Feb-14-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Armando Reyes, T:209.596.3023, plaintiff appearing on his own behalf, by Court Call;
Emily G. Genge, Esq. of BURNHAM BROWN, T:510.835.6819, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF ARMANDO REYES' Motion For Order To Temporarily Stay All Legal Proceedings Pending The Resolution Ot The Emergency Appeal By California Court Of Appeals, as follows:

Plaintiff Armando Reyes' motion for order to temporarily stay all legal proceedings pending the resolution of his "emergency appeal" to the Court of Appeal is denied. Plaintiff has filed notices of appeal from orders dated December 23, 2019 and January 3, 2020 denying his motions to seal his birth date and medical records. However, those orders are not appealable, and Plaintiff has not acted diligently to file a petition for writ of mandate seeking prompt review of either order. Plaintiff has made no showing that he will suffer any harm, much less irreparable harm, if the current case is not stayed, particularly given that he himself revealed his birth date and medical records. In addition, his motion is based on inapplicable authority (Code Civ. Proc., 404.5 and Cal. R. Ct. 3.315 refers to actions that are the subject of a coordination petition). Finally, Plaintiff's contention that the stay should be granted "to prevent further law and motion practice pending this appeal [sic]" cannot be taken seriously, since he has been largely responsible for the burdens placed on the Court and defendant by the motion practice in this comparatively simple case. Since the orders in question were entered, Plaintiff has filed numerous additional discovery motions, which are currently set for hearing on February 25 and various dates in March.

Order signed in open court.

In addition, the Court shall issue a written order (1) taking all pending discovery motions off calendar, (2) assigning a judge pro tem to preside over an informal discovery conference or an informal discovery hearing and (3) setting a hearing date for the parties to meet for an IDC.

[Refer to court reporter's transcript for the Court's complete ruing.]

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Seal Highly-Confidential Medical Records Submitted In Violation Of California Federal-Law And Req. For Hardcore Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Armando Reyes, T:209.596.3023, self-represented plaintiff and moving party, appearing on his own behalf:
Emily G Genge, Esq. of Burnham Brown, T:510.444.6800 ext.6767, for defendant/opposing party, on Court Call.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. After argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF ARMANDO REYES' Motion For Order To Seal Highly-Confidential Medical Records Submitted In Violation Of California Federal-Law And Req. For Hardcore Sanctions, as follows:

Plaintiff's motion to seal is denied. Both parties' requests for sanctions are denied.

"Unless confidentiality is required by law, court records are presumed to be open." (Cal. Rules of Court, Rule 2.550(c).) Records may be sealed only if the court "expressly finds" that "(1) There exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; [] (2) The overriding interest supports sealing the record; [] (3) A substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; [] (4) The proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and [] (5) No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest." (Rule 2.550(d).) "The burden of presenting information . . . is logically placed upon the party seeking the sealing of the documents, who is presumptively in the best position to know what disclosures will harm him and how." (H.B. Fuller Co. v. Doe (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 879, 894.)

Plaintiff fails to meet this burden, instead summarily claiming that he has an overriding interest in keeping the information from the public and that he would be prejudiced if this information was made public for anyone to see. (Plf.'s MPA 8-9.) The contents of the 12 pages at issue are medical chart notes all related to Mr. Reye's alleged ankle injury-an injury Mr. Reyes placed in controversy by bringing this personal injury action-and do not contain particularly sensitive information. Further, Plaintiff himself attached the chart notes to his demand letter to defendant, and did not object to their production by his medical providers. Finally, Plaintiff admits that sealing would "not prevent defendants from either discovering relevant information about Plaintiff Reye's medical condition . . . nor will it bar them from producing these documents in the evidence portion of the trial." (Plf.'s MPA 6.) Plaintiff has not shown that the requirements of the Rule are met.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion, Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Starbucks Corporation And Jen Lu To (1) Further Respond To Plaintiff'S Request For Admissions And For (2) An Award Of Serious Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-22-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Armando Reyes, T:209.596.3023, plaintiff/moving party, appearing on his own behalf, by Court Call;
Emily G. Genge, Esq. of Burnham Brown, T:510.835.6819, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. After argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF ARMANDO REYES' Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Starbucks Corporation And Jen Lu To (1) Further Respond To Plaintiff'S Request For Admissions And For (2) An Award Of Serious Monetary Sanctions, as follows:

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff's motion for an order compelling Defendant Starbucks Corporation and Jen Lu To to further respond to Plaintiff's request for admissions and for an award of monetary sanctions is denied. The motion is untimely. (Code Civ. Proc. 2033.290(c) [unless notice of motion for an order compelling a further response to requests for admission is given within 45 days of the service of the verified response, the requesting party waives any right to compel further response to the requests for admissions]; Sexton v. Superior Court (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 1403, 1410 [the statutory 45-day limitation imposed by the Discovery Act is mandatory, and "renders the court without authority to rule on motions to compel other than to deny them"]; see also Stover v. Bruntz (2017) 12 Cal.App.5th 19, 30-31 [self-represented litigants are required to comply with the time limitations for discovery and discovery motions imposed by the Discovery Act].) Defendant's request for sanctions is denied.

Order signed in open court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reclassify Case
Off Calendar Sep-27-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Armando Reyes, Esq. 209-596-3023 for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Emily Genge, Esq. 510-444-6800 of Burnham Brown for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT STARBUCKS CORPORATION, JEN LU'S Motion To Reclassify Case.

Defendants Starbucks Corporation and Jen Lu's motion to reclassify the case to limited jurisdiction is denied. As defendants concede, the test for their motion is whether damages in the jurisdictional amount are "virtually unattainable." (Ytuarte v. Sup. Ct. (2005) 266, 276-77.) Damages for pain and suffering are "not subject to precise pretrial measurement," so it is particularly difficult to win a reclassification motion in a case like this one. (Id.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Starbucks Corporation And Jen Lu To Further Respond To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories
Off Calendar Aug-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Armando Reyes, 209-596-3023, Plaintiff in Pro Per through Court Call.
Emily Genge, Esq 510-835-6819 of Burnham Brown for Defendant.

Both sides stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ARMANDO REYES' Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Starbucks Corporation And Jen Lu To Further Respond To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. The motion of plaintiff Armando Reyes to compel further responses to interrogatories is DENIED for failure to satisfactorily establish he met the statutory meet and confer requirements prior to filing the motion. A motion to compel further responses to interrogatories must be accompanied by a meet and confer declaration that states facts showing the moving party made a reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution of each issue presented by the motion. (CCP 2016.040, 2030.300.)

The sanctions requests of both parties are denied. However, the Court admonishes plaintiff to familiarize himself with the requirements of discovery motion practice to avoid the risk of incurring sanctions in the future due to noncompliance with the rules concerning such motions. (See e.g., CCP 2023.010 (failure to confer is a misuse of the discovery process) and CCP 2023.020 (monetary sanctions for failure to confer).)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Vestal, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-07-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Armando Reyes, Tel: 209.596.3023, plaintiff appearing on his own behalf, by Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: Plaintiff argued the Court's tentative ruling. The Court clarified and explained the tentative ruling to the self-represented plaintiff. After hearing, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF ARMANDO REYES' DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT, as follows:

Plaintiff Reyes' demurrer to answer is off calendar. First, the demurrer is untimely under Code of Civil Procedure, Sec. 430.40(b) ["[a] party who has filed a complaint ... may, within 10 days after service of the answer to his pleading, demur to the answer."] Here, defendants served the answer on April 8, 2019 and plaintiff filed the demurrer on May 16, 2019. Second, and more importantly, there is no declaration showing that the parties met and conferred regarding the alleged defects in the answer before this demurrer was filed. (See Code Civ. Proc., Sec. 430.41.)

The Court signed the proposed order submitted by counsel for defendant Starbucks, as required by the tentative ruling.
